bull_thesis: Green Dot is in a binding, definitive take-private/restructuring: CommerceOne acquires GDOT and combines Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company (NewCo), while Smith Ventures buys the fintech/embedded-finance business for $690M cash. Per-share consideration is $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares, which management implies is worth $14.23 (low TBV multiple) to $19.18 (high) at close. The shareholder vote is 2026-06-23 (imminent, board-recommended, no competing bid or activist no-vote campaign), and close is expected by end of Q2-2026. Live price ~$12.80 sits BELOW even the low-end $14.23 resolved value, so the deal closing alone delivers ~+11% gross in days-to-weeks, with optionality to +50% ($19.18) if NewCo re-rates to the upper TBV multiple. The cash leg ($8.11, 63% of the low-end value) is contractually fixed; standalone fundamentals are healthy (Q1-26 rev +17% YoY, NI +109%, EPS beat $0.30), so even a break reverts to recent unaffected levels rather than a distressed mark. Conditions hold if: the vote passes (near-certain) and the Fed/OCC bank-holding-company formation approval clears on the Q2 timeline.
bear_thesis: The consideration is only 78% cash; ~22% is 0.2215 shares of an UNLISTED, newly-formed NewCo bank holding company whose realized tangible-book multiple, post-close liquidity, and combined credit/deposit quality are unknown — so the 'value' could land at the $14.23 low end (or below if NewCo trades poorly out of the gate), not the $19.18 headline. The genuine residual risk is the bank-regulatory gate: forming a new BHC requires Federal Reserve / OCC / state approval, which can slip the timeline past Q2 (SOFT-extension risk) or, in the tail, fail. Three NY strike-suits (Phillips, Richardson, Zaccagnino) and multiple firm investigations create deal-tax noise. If the deal breaks, GDOT reverts toward the unaffected/52-wk-low zone (~$11.55-$11.80, tail to $9.31), a -8% to worse outcome. The standalone franchise is a commoditized, payments-disintermediation-exposed prepaid/BaaS issuer with no durable moat, so there is no compounding safety net if the catalyst evaporates.
sop_bull: Win value = full deal consideration realized toward management's range. $8.11 cash + 0.2215 x NewCo. At the high-end TBV multiple management cites, blended ~$19.18/share (+50% vs live $12.80). Base-win (low-end of management's own range, conservative TBV mark) = $14.23/share (+11%). Bull SoP anchor: $16.50-$19.18 if NewCo re-rates; conservative win = $14.23.
sop_bear: Verified downside floor = deal-break / standalone reversion. GDOT loses the stock-component optionality and reverts to recent unaffected trading near the 50-day low / pre-spread zone ~$11.55-$11.80 (52-wk low $9.31 is the distressed tail, unlikely given stable Q1-26 fundamentals). Working bear floor: ~$11.80 (-7.8% vs live $12.80). The hard cash leg ($8.11) only floors the consideration if the deal closes; on a break it is the standalone equity, not $8.11, that sets the mark.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. Assign ~88% to deal close at the conservative low-end value $14.23 (vote 06-23 near-certain; bank-regulatory approval the only real gate, no slippage announced) and ~12% to a break reverting to the ~$11.80 floor: 0.88*14.23 + 0.12*11.80 = $13.94, round to ~$13.95. This excludes the $19.18 high-end NewCo re-rate optionality (treated as free upside, not booked in base). Components: $8.11 hard cash + 0.2215 NewCo stock leg marked conservatively at the low-end TBV multiple = $14.23 win; $11.80 break floor.
risk_reward: Upside-to-target +11.2% ($12.80 -> $14.23 low-end) vs downside-to-floor -7.8% ($12.80 -> $11.80) = ~1.4:1 to the conservative target, rising to 3-6:1 if NewCo re-rates toward the $19.18 high-end. Asymmetry is favorable and the catalyst is days away.
catalyst_summary: Binding definitive merger: CommerceOne acquires GDOT + Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company; Smith Ventures buys the fintech business for $690M cash. Consideration $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares (mgmt-implied $14.23-$19.18). DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08; shareholder special meetings 2026-06-23; expected close Q2-2026 (by ~06-30) pending Fed/OCC/state bank-regulatory approval. Live $12.80 sits below the $14.23 low-end resolved value — a genuine PENDING_HARD arb discount, not trading through terms. Strike-suits already mooted via supplemental disclosures.
dated_milestone: Shareholder vote (GDOT + CommerceOne special meetings) 2026-06-23; expected deal close in Q2-2026 (by ~2026-06-30) subject to final bank-regulatory approval.
forcing_function: The 2026-06-23 shareholder vote (binary, board-recommended, no competing bid) followed by the bank-regulatory close in Q2-2026 forces the spread to collapse to the deal value. Vote pass -> close -> $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo distributed; that is the hard trigger that re-rates the stub from $12.80 to >=$14.23.
consensus_delta: The sell-side carries a 'Hold' rating with a ~$14.75 consensus price target and treats GDOT as an ambiguous standalone payments name (range $14.25-$18 PT). That framing is stale: the company is no longer a going-concern compounding story — it is a near-certain, dated deal-completion with a vote 24 hours out. The market is leaving an ~11% spread to the low-end deal value ($14.23) and ignoring the embedded NewCo re-rate optionality to $19.18, because the stock-component and the unlisted-NewCo mark scare generalist holders into discounting the whole consideration to roughly the cash leg plus a thin premium. The expectations error: pricing residual deal-break/regulatory risk at ~12% when the vote is board-recommended, uncontested, the only real gate is a routine (if slip-prone) BHC formation approval, and the strike-suits are already mooted by supplemental disclosures.
valley_of_death: Near-term (next 1-9 weeks) the only genuine hazard is timeline slip on the Fed/OCC/state bank-holding-company formation approval pushing close past Q2-2026 (a SOFT-extension that would carry the spread but delay the cash). No cash-burn hump, no debt-maturity wall, and standalone FCF/earnings are healthy (Q1-26 NI +109% YoY), so there is no fundamental decay that punishes the stub before the catalyst. The disclosure strike-suits (Phillips/Richardson/Zaccagnino) are deal-tax noise — no injunction sought, claims mooted via supplemental disclosures — and do not threaten the close. The real tail is a regulatory denial of the new BHC, which would break the deal and revert GDOT to ~$11.80 (-8%).
positioning_washout: Already largely de-risked: arb desks hold the spread, and the remaining generalist/passive base faces a clean cash-plus-stock close rather than a dividend cut or pivot that forces mechanical liquidation. The one mechanical event is that holders who do not want unlisted NewCo paper (index funds, certain mandates that cannot hold the new BHC) will sell into the close, which partly explains why the spread to $14.23 persists — that selling pressure is the source of the discount, not a sign the deal is at risk. Post-close, NewCo's initial trading could see forced selling from holders who only wanted GDOT, a separate (and tradable) dynamic from the arb itself.
moderator_conclusion: GDOT is a textbook held-seat deal-completion arb at the optimal entry point. The catalyst is PENDING_HARD: a binding definitive merger ($8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares, mgmt-implied $14.23-$19.18), a board-recommended shareholder vote 2026-06-23 (tomorrow), and an expected Q2-2026 close, with the live price ~$12.80 sitting BELOW even the conservative low-end deal value of $14.23. That is precisely the 5/A configuration — dated/binding catalyst inside the window, price well below the event-resolved target, and a defined ~$11.80 break floor that bounds the loss to ~-8% against +11% to the low-end target and up to +50% to the $19.18 high-end. Do NOT cap this like a priced compounder: the moat is NONE and the standalone is payments-disintermediation-exposed, but that is by design — the catalyst carries the thesis. The one real risk is the bank-regulatory BHC-formation gate slipping the timeline (carries the spread, doesn't kill it) plus the unlisted-NewCo stock-component mark, which is why I set sop_fair_value at $13.95 (88/12 weighted) rather than booking the high-end. value_conviction is 3, not 5: stripped of the catalyst this is an optically-fair, no-moat franchise — the asymmetry is entirely event-sourced. Verdict A, conviction 5 on the event asymmetry; HELD seat affirmed, catalyst imminent.
interrogator_dossier:
# GDOT — Green Dot Corp — Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven / Merger-Arb)

**Frame:** This is NOT a business-quality or compounding judgment. Green Dot is a structurally-pressured prepaid-card / BaaS issuer with a thin, eroding standalone franchise. The thesis is the SPREAD between the live price (~$12.80, 2026-06-22) and the event-resolved deal value, gated on the merger firing, with a defined cash floor that bounds the loss. The catalyst carries the thesis.

## 1. THE EVENT — is it real, dated, binding?

**Real and binding.** On 2026-11-23 Green Dot announced definitive agreements (Merger Agreement + Separation Agreement) under which: CommerceOne Financial Corp. acquires Green Dot and combines Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company (NewCo); Smith Ventures LLC simultaneously acquires the fintech/embedded-finance business from NewCo for $690M cash. Per-share consideration: $8.11 cash + 0.2215 shares of NewCo. Former GDOT holders own ~72% of NewCo, CommerceOne ~28%. Aggregate value $825M-$1.1B (incl. ~$470M cash). Management-stated implied per-share value range: $14.23 (low TBV multiple) - $19.18 (high) at close.

**Dated and imminent.** DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08. Special meeting / shareholder vote 2026-06-23 (tomorrow). Expected close Q2-2026 (by ~06-30), contingent on final regulatory sign-off.

**Conditions:** shareholder approval (both special meetings 06-23), required bank regulatory approvals (Fed/OCC/state — a BHC formation, so the regulatory step is the genuine residual risk, not antitrust), and customary closing conditions.

## 2. THE VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR

The floor is NOT the $8.11 cash alone, because ~22% of consideration is unlisted NewCo stock. Cash leg $8.11 is contractually fixed/hard. Stock leg 0.2215 x NewCo marked conservatively floors the blended DEAL value at management's low-end $14.23. A break floor (deal fails, GDOT reverts standalone) sits near the recent unaffected / 50-day-low zone ~$11.55-$11.80 (52-wk low $9.31 is the tail). Working downside floor for the held seat: ~$11.80 — deal-break reversion, comfortably below live $12.80 and well below the $14.23 low-end deal value. Bounds loss at roughly -8% vs upside-to-low-end +11% ($14.23) and up to +50% ($19.18) on a high-end NewCo re-rate.

## 3. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

RED — Stock-component risk: ~22% of consideration is 0.2215 shares of an unlisted NewCo BHC; post-close liquidity, realized TBV multiple, and combined credit/deposit profile unknown. The 'target' is the LOW end of management's own range; do not credit $19.18 as base.
RED — Bank-regulatory gate: requires Fed/OCC/state approval to FORM a new BHC; the one condition that can slip past Q2 (SOFT-extension risk), though no slippage announced.
YELLOW — Strike-suit overhang (benign): three NY disclosure-deficiency suits (Phillips 05-27, Richardson 05-28, Zaccagnino 06-08) + Schall/Wohl & Fruchter/Kaskela investigations. Company already issued supplemental merger disclosures — textbook moot-the-claim pattern; no injunction sought/granted.
GREEN — No financing condition of note on the cash leg; binding gate is regulatory + vote, both imminent.
GREEN — Standalone fundamentals stable not collapsing: Q1-26 rev +17% YoY to $656.2M, NI +109% YoY to $53.7M, EPS beat $0.30 — supports the break-floor near unaffected levels.
GREEN — Trading BELOW low-end deal value, not through terms: live $12.80 < $14.23 — a genuine arb discount (PENDING_HARD), not an ARB name at/through the offer.

## 4. SPREAD ECONOMICS

Live $12.80 (2026-06-22, intraday $12.76-$12.92). Target (low-end deal value, base) $14.23 -> +11.2% gross, captured on close. Floor (break/standalone) ~$11.80 -> -7.8%. Asymmetry ~1.4-1.6:1 to low-end target; 3-6:1 if NewCo re-rates toward $19.18. Catalyst PENDING_HARD: dated (vote 06-23, close Q2-26), binding (definitive agreements), real asymmetry, defined floor.

## 5. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

EVENT-DRIVEN / DEAL-COMPLETION, not a franchise judgment. Single most important thing to weigh: the stock-component + bank-regulatory tail — the $8.11 cash leg is hard, but ~22% of value rides on an unlisted NewCo whose mark sets whether realized value lands at $14.23 or $19.18, and the Fed/OCC formation approval is the one gate that could slip. The 06-23 vote is near-certain (board-recommended, no competing bid/activist no-vote); strike-suits are mooted noise. Clean held-seat arb, catalyst imminent, live price below low-end resolved value. Moat NONE (commoditized prepaid/BaaS, payments-disintermediation-exposed) but irrelevant; the catalyst carries the thesis. Secular threat manageable for the event value. Trajectory STABLE.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: The bull's load-bearing claim — live ~$12.80 sits BELOW a 'resolved' deal value of $14.23, delivering +11% on close in days-to-weeks — is false. Only $8.11 of the consideration is hard cash; the other ~$6.12/sh is 0.2215 shares of an UNLISTED, never-traded NewCo bank holding company. Per Citi's fairness opinion (DEFM14A / Form 425), $14.23-$19.18 is an ILLUSTRATIVE range that credits that stub at 1.00x-1.81x tangible book; NewCo pro-forma TBV is only ~$490M (~$355M / ~$6.12 per GDOT share to former holders). The $14.23 'conservative low end' is NOT conservative — it marks a brand-new small-cap bank (born of a forced restructuring of a payments-disintermediation-exposed prepaid/BaaS issuer, with admitted forced index/mandate selling at the open) at FULL 1.00x book. At a realistic newly-listed-bank discount (~0.7-0.8x TBV) the stub is worth ~$3.7-4.9 and realized value is ~$11.8-$13.0 — i.e. the market's $12.80 IS the correct mark, not an arb discount. There is no genuine spread to capture; the stock is at fair, not below it. Compounding this: the bull's 'close by ~06-30, days-to-weeks' timeline is wrong — the 06-23 vote only clears the SHAREHOLDER condition, while the binding gate (Fed / Utah DFI / ASBD approval to FORM a new BHC) is multi-month; the merger agreement's outside date is Nov 23, 2026 PLUS a 90-day auto-extension. Single-binary stub with no hard floor (on a break the holder owns standalone no-moat equity, not the $8.11 cash), trading-through on a mark-to-model unlisted share. Default REFUTED stands.

catalyst.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote (GDOT + CommerceOne special meetings) 2026-06-23; expected deal close in Q2-2026 (by ~2026-06-30) subject to final bank-regulatory approval.
catalyst.binding_reason: Binding definitive merger: CommerceOne acquires GDOT + Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company; Smith Ventures buys the fintech business for $690M cash. Consideration $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares (mgmt-implied $14.23-$19.18). DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08; shareholder special meetings 2026-06-23; expected close Q2-2026 (by ~06-30) pending Fed/OCC/state bank-regulatory approval. Live $12.80 sits below the $14.23 low-end resolved value — a genuine PENDING_HARD arb discount, not trading through terms. Strike-suits already mooted via supplemental disclosures.

bull_thesis: Green Dot (about $13.36 a share as of June 26, 2026) is a late-stage merger arbitrage where most of the deal risk is already gone. On November 23, 2025 the company agreed to break itself up: CommerceOne Financial buys Green Dot Bank into a new, publicly traded bank holding company, and Smith Ventures buys and takes private the non-bank fintech operations. Each share converts into a fixed payout of $8.11 in cash plus 0.2215 shares of the new CommerceOne entity, worth roughly $14.23 in total at current marks. The single biggest hurdle is behind it: on June 23, 2026 shareholders approved overwhelmingly (99% of votes cast, about 72% of shares outstanding). All that remains is bank-regulatory clearance from the Federal Reserve, the Alabama State Banking Department, and the Utah Department of Financial Institutions, expected in the third quarter of 2026. If the deal closes on schedule, holders collect the spread from today's price up to the deal value, a low-drama event-driven gain that does not depend on the business improving. The structure is conventional, the cash component is large and contractual, and two separate buyers wanted the pieces, all of which point to a high probability of completion.
bear_thesis: The reward here is thin and the path is not risk-free. The spread from today's price to the roughly $14.23 deal value is only about 6 to 7 percent, so the easy money has already been made as the deal de-risked through the shareholder vote. Bank-merger approval is genuinely discretionary: the Federal Reserve has been slower and more demanding on bank deals, and any one of three regulators can push the timeline past the third quarter of 2026, which would erode the annualized return and tie up capital. Part of the payout is stock in the new CommerceOne entity (0.2215 shares per Green Dot share), so the realized value floats rather than being fully fixed in cash. If the deal were to break, the stock would likely fall toward a standalone value near $11.80 and could overshoot toward its 52-week low around $9.31, meaning the downside to a break exceeds the upside to the target. The underlying franchise is being dismantled precisely because it is structurally challenged, so there is little franchise value to fall back on beyond the cash and the bid interest in the pieces.
consensus_delta: There is little hidden disagreement left to exploit. With the shareholder vote already won and terms fixed, the market is correctly pricing this as a high-probability deal close: the stock at about $13.36 sits just below the roughly $14.23 deal value, leaving a normal arbitrage spread of 6 to 7 percent that reflects time-to-close and a small probability of a regulatory hiccup. The street's analyst price targets clustering around $16 are stale relative to the deal cap and should be discounted; the relevant value ceiling is the deal consideration, not a standalone-company target. The genuine edge is narrow and entirely about the odds and timing of regulatory clearance, not a mispriced fundamental thesis.
forcing_function: Receipt of the final bank-regulatory approvals (Federal Reserve plus the Alabama and Utah state banking regulators), which triggers the deal close and the payout of $8.11 cash plus 0.2215 new-CommerceOne shares per Green Dot share, expected during the third quarter of 2026.
valley_of_death: The main near-term risk over the next three to nine months is regulatory slippage rather than cash burn or a maturity wall. Bank-M&A approvals from the Federal Reserve and two state regulators can extend past the targeted third quarter of 2026; a delay would not break the deal but would lower the annualized return and lock up capital longer. The secondary risk is the floating stock leg: because part of the payout is shares in the new CommerceOne entity, weakness in that entity's implied value before close would trim the realized payout. A small but real tail is an outright break, which would drop the stock toward the $11.80 standalone floor and possibly overshoot toward the $9.31 52-week low.
positioning_washout: No meaningful forced-selling dynamic is in play. The shareholder base is already largely event-driven and arbitrage-oriented following the November 2025 announcement and the June 2026 vote; there is no dividend cut or index reconstitution forcing mechanical liquidation. The eventual washout is the deal close itself, when cash plus new-entity shares are distributed and the Green Dot line ceases to exist, at which point holders either take the new bank stock or sell it.
moderator_conclusion: This is a clean, late-stage merger arbitrage in which the hardest discretionary step, the shareholder vote, was won overwhelmingly on June 23, 2026, leaving only conventional bank-regulatory clearance (Federal Reserve plus Alabama and Utah state regulators) before a Q3 2026 close. The catalyst is real, dated, and binding, and the $8.11 cash component gives the floor real substance, which is why this earns a defined-risk arb treatment rather than a pass. But the spread has already compressed to about 6 to 7 percent, and the downside to a break floor near $11.80 actually exceeds the upside to the roughly $14.23 deal value from today's $13.36, so the asymmetry is only mildly favorable. That argues for a modest, defined-risk position rather than an aggressive swing: a fine low-volatility arb to own into the close, but not the wide mispricing the funnel is hunting for. Wait for either a wider spread on a transient regulatory-timing scare or simply size it small and clip the deal. Conviction 3; value conviction 3, set by the margin of safety to the verified $11.80 cash-anchored floor, not by any franchise moat (there is none, the business is being sold for parts).
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. Assigning roughly an 85% chance the deal closes at about $14.23 and a 15% chance it breaks to about $11.80 gives a blended fair value near $13.86 per share. The cash component ($8.11) anchors the floor; the stock component (0.2215 new-entity shares) and the regulatory clearance carry the remaining upside.
risk_reward: Roughly 0.6 to 1 from current levels: about $0.87 of upside to the $14.23 target versus about $1.56 of downside to the $11.80 break floor. Late-stage arb with a thin, de-risked spread, so the asymmetry is modest, not wide.
interrogator_dossier:
# Green Dot Corporation (GDOT) — Event Dossier

**Setup type:** Merger arbitrage / deal completion. This is a special situation, not a quality compounder. The thesis is the spread between today's price (about $13.36 as of June 26, 2026) and the agreed deal value (roughly $14.23 per share), gated on one remaining event: bank regulators signing off so the transaction can close.

## 1. THE EVENT — is it real, dated, and binding?

It is real and largely locked. On November 23, 2025, Green Dot announced a strategic break-up: CommerceOne Financial will acquire Green Dot Bank and roll it into a new, publicly traded bank holding company, while Smith Ventures buys and takes private Green Dot's non-bank financial-technology operations. The two pieces are wired together — the new bank becomes the fintech's exclusive issuing bank.

The terms are fixed (this is what makes it arbitrage rather than open-ended speculation): each Green Dot share converts into **$8.11 in cash plus 0.2215 shares of the new CommerceOne holding company**. The cash piece alone is $8.11; the stock piece carries the rest of the value.

The deal has cleared the biggest discretionary hurdle. On **June 23, 2026**, both Green Dot and CommerceOne shareholders voted to approve, with 99% of Green Dot shares voted in favor — about 72% of all shares outstanding. A definitive proxy (DEFM14A) and the new entity's registration (Form 424B3, filed by Compass Sub North) are on file with the SEC.

What is left is the regulatory close, expected in the **third quarter of 2026**. The pending approvals are the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, the Alabama State Banking Department, and the Utah Department of Financial Institutions — the standard package for a bank-holding-company change of control. There is no longer a financing contingency or a shareholder-vote risk; the catalyst is a dated, binding regulatory clearance, not a soft promise.

## 2. CREDIBILITY OF THE PARTIES

Credibility here is about deal certainty, not management's operating skill (the operating franchise is being dismantled and sold, so its quality no longer carries the thesis). The relevant questions are whether regulators will approve and whether the parties stay committed.

Positives: the shareholder vote is done and overwhelming; the structure is conventional (a bank holding company buying a chartered bank, with the fintech carved out to a private buyer); both companies have signed, filed, and publicly reaffirmed the Q3 2026 timeline. Bank deals of this size routinely clear the Fed and state regulators when there are no obvious antitrust or safety-and-soundness red flags, and a roughly $1.1B fintech/community-bank tie-up is not the kind of deal that draws a heavy-handed second look.

Risks: bank-regulatory approval is genuinely discretionary and can slip. The Fed has been slower and more demanding on bank M&A in recent years, and any one of three regulators can extend the timeline. There is also a Scott+Scott "investigation" notice circulating — these are routine plaintiff-firm solicitations on announced deals and almost never derail a transaction shareholders have already approved, but they are worth noting as background noise rather than a real obstacle.

## 3. THE FLOOR — what backstops the price if the event fails?

The downside is bounded but not bulletproof. The $8.11 cash component is contractual and large relative to the share price, which anchors a meaningful chunk of value. The verified downside floor used here is about $11.80 — roughly where the stock would settle on a deal break, reflecting Green Dot's standalone value as a still-cash-generative (if structurally challenged) fintech-plus-bank, supported by the cash on the balance sheet and the businesses that were independently bid for. That floor is below today's price, so a break would hurt, but the cash component and the fact that two separate buyers wanted the pieces limit how far it can fall. The 52-week low was $9.31, set before the deal momentum, which marks a realistic worst-case stress level if the deal collapsed and sentiment overshot.

## 4. THE ASYMMETRY

- Live price: about $13.36
- Deal value target: about $14.23 (cash plus the value of the 0.2215 stock piece)
- Downside floor on a break: about $11.80

That is roughly $0.87 of upside to the target against $1.56 of downside to the floor from current levels — a spread reward-to-risk near 1.6 to 1, with the key sweetener being that the hard work (shareholder approval) is already behind it and only a dated regulatory clearance remains. The asymmetry is modest in absolute terms because the spread has already tightened as the deal de-risked; this is a late-stage arb, not a wide-open mispricing.

## 5. RED / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 Shareholder vote passed overwhelmingly (June 23, 2026) — the single largest deal risk is retired.
🟢 Fixed terms, definitive filings on record, cash component is large and contractual.
🟢 Dated close (Q3 2026) with a conventional, approvable structure.
🔴 Three separate bank regulators must still approve; bank-M&A timelines can slip a quarter or more.
🔴 The stock-component value floats with the new CommerceOne entity, so the realized payout is not perfectly fixed.
🔴 Spread is already thin (~6-7%), so most of the easy money is gone; downside to the break floor exceeds upside to the target.

## 6. VERDICT FOR THE DIRECTOR

This is a clean, late-stage merger arbitrage with the vote already won and only regulatory clearance outstanding into Q3 2026. The catalyst is real, dated, and binding, and the floor is reasonable thanks to the cash component — but the spread has compressed enough that the reward-to-risk is only mildly favorable. Size it as a defined-risk arb position, not a high-conviction asymmetric swing.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
